recommendation.reasoning: The decision to not pursue the PageSpeed product is primarily driven by critical performance regressions identified in both dashboard load time and core metrics, which pose significant risks to user experience and overall product viability [risk-001, risk-002]. Additionally, the lack of automated performance regression detection further exacerbates these issues, indicating that the product cannot meet essential performance requirements and may lead to customer dissatisfaction and competitive disadvantage [risk-004, competitive-001].

executive_summary:
The product under consideration, PageSpeed, aims to enhance the performance of our dashboard and API response times, addressing significant customer pain points related to load times and latency. The opportunity for improvement is underscored by critical findings, including a regression in core metrics and dashboard load times, which have been flagged as top priorities by our customers [customer-001], [competitive-001]. The analysis of 38 findings has revealed that the current state of the product presents substantial challenges that need to be addressed before any further investment can be justified.

The analysis has identified several critical risks that have led to a failed risk gate, including a critical performance regression in dashboard load time [risk-001], a regression in core metrics [risk-002], and a lack of automated performance regression detection [risk-004]. These blockers indicate that the product is not only underperforming but also lacks the necessary mechanisms to detect and address performance issues proactively. While there are opportunities for optimization, such as improving API latency and addressing database migration impacts, the trade-offs associated with pursuing this product at this time outweigh the potential benefits.

Based on the findings and the associated risks, the recommendation is to not pursue the PageSpeed product at this time, with a confidence level of 0.85. The immediate next steps for the team should include a thorough review of the identified blockers and risks, followed by a strategic decision to either allocate resources towards resolving these critical issues or to redirect efforts towards alternative initiatives that align better with our performance goals and customer expectations.

key_findings.title: Critical Regression in Core Metrics
key_findings.description: The recent regression in core page load metrics indicates a significant risk to PageSpeed's performance reputation. This could lead to customer dissatisfaction and attrition if not addressed promptly.
recommendations: Implement automated performance benchmarking in CI to catch regressions early.
assumptions: The regression directly affects user experience and customer retention.

key_findings.title: Critical Dashboard Load Time Regression
key_findings.description: The dashboard load time has regressed significantly from 1.2s to 4.8s after the v3.2 deployment, severely impacting user experience, especially during incidents.
recommendations: Prioritize performance fixes for the dashboard load time.
assumptions: Users rely on the dashboard for real-time monitoring during incidents.

key_findings.title: API Latency Degradation
key_findings.description: API response times have degraded significantly, impacting automated alerting systems and overall user experience.
recommendations: Investigate and resolve the query planner regression in PostgreSQL.
assumptions: Users depend on timely API responses for alerting and monitoring.

key_findings.title: Critical Performance Regression in Dashboard Load Time
key_findings.description: The recent deployment of v3.2 has caused a significant regression in dashboard load times due to unoptimized SVG rendering in the new charting library. This impacts user experience and could lead to customer dissatisfaction.
recommendations: Optimize SVG rendering in the charting library.
Consider rolling back to the previous version of the library if optimizations are not feasible.
assumptions: The new charting library is the primary cause of the regression.

key_findings.title: Database Migration Impact on API Response Times
key_findings.description: The migration from PostgreSQL 14 to 16 has introduced a regression in API response times due to a query planner issue. This needs to be addressed to restore performance levels.
recommendations: Investigate and optimize the query planner settings.
Consider reverting to PostgreSQL 14 if the issue cannot be resolved quickly.
assumptions: The query planner regression is the root cause of the API latency increase.

key_findings.title: North Star Metric: Dashboard Load Time
key_findings.description: The North Star metric for PageSpeed is the 95th percentile load time of the dashboard, which directly impacts user experience and satisfaction.
recommendations: Focus on optimizing the dashboard load time to improve user experience.
assumptions: Improving dashboard load time will enhance user satisfaction and reduce support tickets.

key_findings.title: Instrumentation Gap: Performance Regression Detection
key_findings.description: Currently, there is no automated performance regression detection in the CI pipeline, which could prevent future regressions.
recommendations: Develop and implement a performance benchmarking suite in the CI pipeline.
assumptions: Automated performance checks will catch regressions before they reach production.

key_findings.title: Dashboard Load Time Optimization
key_findings.description: Optimize the dashboard load time to ensure it does not exceed 2 seconds for 95th percentile load times.
recommendations: Investigate SVG rendering optimizations in the new charting library.
assumptions: The new charting library can be optimized for performance.

key_findings.title: API Latency Improvement
key_findings.description: Reduce API response times to ensure p95 latency does not exceed 200ms.
recommendations: Analyze and optimize the query planner for time-series aggregation.
assumptions: Database query optimizations can be implemented.

key_findings.title: WebSocket Memory Leak Fix
key_findings.description: Resolve the memory leak issue in WebSocket connections to prevent server crashes.
recommendations: Conduct heap profiling to identify and fix memory leaks.
assumptions: The memory leak can be identified and fixed in the WebSocket implementation.
